info.title: Waftpay Payments API
info.description: Process payments, collect funds, and remit payouts with Waftpay. Includes Authentication Token, idempotency, webhooks, and sandbox support.

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: Charge a customer and track status

tags.name: Checkout
tags.description:
Hosted checkout flow for customer payments.

Flow:
1. Ensure the Collections service is enabled for your account.
2. Use `consumer_key` and `consumer_secret` to generate a Bearer token via **Get Authentication Token**.
3. Cache the token and refresh only on expiry.
4. Call **Create Checkout Request** with customer details, amount, and redirect URLs.
5. Use the returned `checkout_url` for redirect or iframe.
6. The `callback_url` receives a payload matching the `CheckoutCallback` schema.

tags.name: Remittance
tags.description: Send payouts to mobile wallets and bank accounts

post.description: Exchange **consumer_key** and **consumer_secret** for a short-lived **Bearer** token.

status.description: Indicates if the request was successful

token.description: JWT bearer token to use in Authorization header

post.description: Create a customer collection request. Provide a unique `transaction.reference` for idempotency. Uses the same authentication and signature flow as payouts.

meta.note: This info is returned as part of the callback

post.description: Initiate a hosted checkout session and receive a `checkout_url` for redirect or iframe. Requires a valid Bearer token from **Get Authentication Token**. The `callback_url` receives the `CheckoutCallback` payload.

get.description: Fetch wallet balances for the authenticated product (collections, payouts, etc.). Optionally filter by currency (ISO code). If omitted, all balances for the product are returned.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: ISO currency code to filter the balance (e.g., KES, UGX, USD).

404.description: Balances not found for provided currency

401.description: Unauthorized (invalid/expired token or wrong product scope)

post.description: Create a remittance request. Provide a unique `transaction.reference` for idempotency. Uses the same Bearer authentication and `X-Custom-Signature` convention as payouts/collections.
Create a refund or reversal request for a collection payment. Returns a request_uuid for status checks.

Query the status of a refund reversal by request_uuid. Returns a standardized APIResponse with status and transaction details when found. If not found, a NOT FOUND-style response is returned in the payload. This endpoint requires a Bearer token.

unauthorizedInBody.summary: Authorization failure represented in body (tenant style)

notFoundInBody.summary: Not found represented in body (tenant style)

post.summary: Send a test webhook to your callback URL
post.description: Receive asynchronous event notifications (payments and payouts). Verify signature via Waftpay-Signature header.

phone_number.description: Customer phone number in MSISDN format (e.g., 2547XXXXXXXX).

expiry.description: Optional expiry timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format. If omitted, the default expiry applies.

strict_amount.description: If true, only full payment is allowed. If false, partial payments are allowed.

payment_reference.description: Your internal invoice or payment reference.

msisdn.description: Payer phone number in MSISDN format (e.g., 2547XXXXXXXX).

callback_url.description: URL for payment status callback notifications.

success_redirect_url.description: URL to redirect on successful payment.

fail_redirect_url.description: URL to redirect on failed payment.

meta_data.description: Arbitrary key-value data returned in the callback.

payments.result_description: The service request is processed successfully.

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference (idempotency key). Reuse to safely retry.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code in upper-case, e.g., KES, USD.

service_code.description: Service the client wants to consume (e.g., MPESAB2C).

msisdn.description: Originator phone number in MSISDN format (e.g., 2547XXXXXXXX).

channel.description: Channel used to initiate the payout (e.g., USSD, API).

country.description: Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 like 'KE' or alpha-3 like 'KEN'). Use what's configured on your account.

name.description: Originator name - preferable two names (e.g., Alvin Muriithi

purpose.description: Originator purpose or reason for payout

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference (idempotency key). Reuse to safely retry.

account.description: Recipient account identifier (phone, wallet, bank acct).

destination_code.description: Destination code for the recipient corridor or payout rail.

callback_url.description: HTTPS URL to receive final status callbacks.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata echoed back in callbacks.

meta.note: This info is returned as part of the callback

value.description: Payout with reference TXN213687756272200 was not found

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference (idempotency key). Reuse to safely retry.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code in upper-case, e.g., KES, USD.

service_code.description: Service the client wants to consume (e.g., RMT_WU, MPESA_STK).

msisdn.description: Originator phone number in MSISDN format (e.g., 2547XXXXXXXX).

channel.description: Channel used to initiate the collection (e.g., USSD, API).

purpose.description: Purpose of the collection (e.g., Salary Payment).

invoice_number.description: Optional invoice number associated with the transaction.

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference for recipient context.

account.description: Customer account identifier (phone, wallet, bank account, etc.).

callback_url.description: HTTPS URL to receive final status callbacks.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata echoed back in callbacks.

meta.note: This info is returned as part of the callback

transaction_reference.description: This is the client-generated transaction reference

amount.description: Amount to refund/reverse in major units.

notification_url.description: HTTPS URL to receive final status callbacks.

request_uuid.description: Refund/reversal request UUID returned for status checks.
Refund/reversal request UUID returned when the refund/reversal was created.

RefundReversalResults.description: Provider/platform response fields for a refund reversal query. Fields are optional and may vary by provider.

total_amount.description: Total amount; some providers return labeled strings (e.g., MPESA_KE_MOCK).

time_processed.description: ISO 8601 timestamp; may include fractional seconds.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata echoed back by the platform/provider.

code.description: Application code for the query result (e.g., 102=SUCCESS, 103=FAILED, 401/404 represented in body where applicable).

status.description: Outcome label (e.g., SUCCESS, FAILED, FAILED AUTHORIZATION, NOT FOUND).

oneOf.description: Empty results object for non-success outcomes.

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference (idempotency key). Reuse to safely retry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp in 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss' as provided by client (assumed UTC unless configured).

msisdn.description: Originator phone number in MSISDN format (e.g., 2547XXXXXXXX).

channel.description: Channel used to initiate the remittance (e.g., USSD, API).

country.description: Country code; tenant may use ISO-2 (KE) or ISO-3 (KEN). Use what your account is configured for.

service_provider.description: Sending provider name as used in your corridor (e.g., TELCOM-UK).

id_number.description: Identification number (e.g., Passport number, ID number, DL number).

reference.description: Client-generated unique reference for recipient context.

account.description: Recipient account identifier (phone, wallet, bank account, etc.).

destination_code.description: Destination code for the recipient corridor or payout rail.

callback_url.description: HTTPS URL to receive final status callbacks.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata echoed back in callbacks.

code.description: Application code. Can be a top-level code (e.g., 400.600) or a granular code (e.g., 404.001.000).

status.description: Semantic status, e.g., REJECTED, FAILED, UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, ACCEPTED.

XCustomSignature.name: X-Custom-Signature
XCustomSignature.description: Base64 RSA signature over: transaction.reference + transaction.amount + originator.country + transaction.service_code
